patches.summary: Hand-written Surf-based source clients for OpenTable (GraphQL + SSR) and Tock (REST + SSR), plus a shared session-cookie store imported from local Chrome via kooky.
patches.reason: Both consumer sites sit behind Akamai/Cloudflare bot defense; Surf's Chrome TLS fingerprint clears them. The OpenTable Partner API is gated and Tock has no public REST API, so the only viable path is the consumer-side surface — there's no upstream spec to generate from, so these are net-new packages outside the generator's control. The packages live in `internal/source/{auth,opentable,tock}/` and back the goat/watch/earliest/drift commands.
patches.validated_outcome: Live test: 24 OpenTable cookies + 29 Tock cookies imported via `auth login --chrome`; `auth status` reports both networks logged_in: true. v0.1.1: goja-based JS evaluator (jseval) replaced naive balanced-brace + regex strip; live `goat 'canlis'` now returns Canlis on both networks (Tock id=4891, OT id=6309). v0.1.2: AdaptiveLimiter + typed RateLimitError wired into both source clients (do429Aware helper) — single 429 retry with Retry-After backoff, then surface as hard error so empty-on-throttle stops being indistinguishable from 'no data exists'. OT floor 0.5 req/s, Tock 1.0 req/s; both ramp via OnSuccess after 10 consecutive successes. v0.1.3 (Greptile review): SearchRestaurants default DateTime now computed at call time (today+7d at 19:00); Bootstrap dedupes concurrent fetches via singleflight. v0.1.5: OT RestaurantsAvailability GraphQL persisted-query wired with hash e6b87021ed6e... (from 21Bruce/resolved-bot's documented FindKey). New types AvailabilitySlot/AvailabilityDay/RestaurantAvailability + RestaurantsAvailability() method + RestaurantIDFromQuery() slug→id resolver. v0.1.6: OpenTable bot-detection cooldown awareness — new internal/source/opentable/cooldown.go with typed *BotDetectionError, disk-persisted cooldown at ~/.cache/table-reservation-goat-pp-cli/opentable-cooldown.json, exponential backoff (5/10/20/40/60min cap) per consecutive 403, fast-fail on Bootstrap and do429Aware so SSR/GraphQL/REST paths all benefit, 200 clears the cooldown. Live-tested: first 403 triggers cooldown + typed error, second invocation from disk fast-fails without re-hitting OT. v0.1.9: real Tock per-(date,party,time) availability — reverse-engineered Tock's runtime XHR `POST /api/consumer/calendar/full/v2`, extracted the proto2 schema from the live JS bundle's embedded JSON descriptors (ConsumerFullCalendarV2 → map<businessDay,TicketGroupByDate> → map<date,TicketGroupList> → repeated CalendarTicketGroup with availableTickets/min-max purchase size). New `internal/source/tock/calendar.go` with hand-rolled wire-format walker, `Calendar(slug)` method that bootstraps businessId/JWT/build-number from the venue SSR and POSTs an empty ConsumerCalendarRequest body. Replaces the SSR-metadata-only path that previously could not prove availability. Live-tested: Canlis party=6 on 2026-05-13 returns 3 real bookable times (17:00, 17:30, 19:00) matching the user-confirmed validation case; same query for 2026-05-09 (today) honestly returns no slots, matching the user's screenshot of Canlis sold out for 6. v0.1.10: Akamai cookie freshness — RefreshAkamaiCookies() reads live `_abck`/`bm_*`/`ftc` cookies from Chrome via kooky on each OT client construction, with disk-cache (~/Library/Caches/.../akamai-<domain>.json, 10-min TTL) so subsequent invocations skip the keychain walk; fresh cookies override stale session-snapshot entries. Negative cache (kooky-timeout entries) prevents 10s hangs on every command when keychain access is unavailable. `auth login --chrome` clears the cache so it doubles as a deliberate refresh path. KNOWN LIMITATION: cookie freshness alone does not bypass Akamai's session-integrity check — `_abck` carries internal state that Akamai expects to be updated by their JS sensor payload, which a pure-HTTP client cannot fake. Even freshly-imported cookies still trigger 403s from `/` when Akamai's JS-validation watermark expires. Cross-network commands (goat, earliest, watch tick) degrade gracefully: OT errors land in the per-row Reason or top-level errors[] without breaking other venues' rows. Recovery hint surfaced in BotDetectionError.Error(). v0.1.11: OT bootstrap + cooldown recovery — moved Bootstrap source from `/` (which Akamai 403s on every Surf-Chrome request, instantly setting a 5-min cooldown that prevented any recovery) to `/restaurant/profile/100` (a stable numeric ID Akamai whitelists). GraphQL gqlCall now sends `apollographql-client-name: fe-search`, `x-query-timeout`, and Referer pointing to the bootstrap page (matches what real Chrome sends; some Akamai rules drop GraphQL requests without these). Bootstrap-specific 403s still trigger session cooldown (correct: that's the canary), but op-specific 403s (e.g., Akamai's WAF rule on `opname=RestaurantsAvailability`) now return a per-call BotDetectionError WITHOUT poisoning the session — Autocomplete and other ops keep working on the same client. Result: `goat <name>` and `earliest <slug>` slug→ID resolution work cleanly across both networks; OT venue URLs resolve correctly. KNOWN LIMITATION: Akamai's WAF maintains an opname-allowlist for `/dapi/fe/gql` that blocks `RestaurantsAvailability` even with a fresh CSRF + valid session cookies + correct Referer + apollographql-client-name. The OT GraphQL gateway's persisted-query mechanism (409 Conflict on operationName mismatch) prevents aliasing the operation under a different name. Inline-query fallback is blocked by missing query text in the JS bundles. Real-Chrome path (chromedp) is the documented escape hatch for slot fetches when needed; tracked separately. The earliest's OT row carries the resolved venue URL so users can click through to OpenTable to see slots and book. v0.1.12: chromedp fallback for OT slot fetches — new internal/source/opentable/chrome_avail.go uses chromedp's CDP client to navigate a real Chrome to the OT restaurant page and intercept the page's own RestaurantsAvailability XHR (which Akamai accepts when Chrome runs the JS sensor naturally). Two modes: (a) ATTACH — auto-detects a Chrome listening on http://localhost:9222 (or TABLE_RESERVATION_GOAT_OT_CHROME_DEBUG_URL); routes through the user's actual logged-in Chrome session, guaranteed to bypass Akamai because the request is made by the real browser. (b) SPAWN — falls back to a stealth-spawned headless Chrome with navigator.webdriver/plugins/languages shims, AutomationControlled disabled, real-Chrome UA, warm-up navigation to /restaurant/profile/100 so Akamai's sensor issues fresh _abck/bm_*. Spawn mode currently 403s against OT's WAF (Akamai detects CDP runtime markers); the path is wired so users without remote-debug get a clear error explaining how to switch to attach. Earliest's OT branch auto-falls-back on BotDetectionError. The error message includes the exact Chrome relaunch command so users know how to enable attach mode. v0.1.13: OT GraphQL refresh — captured the live persisted-query hash and variable shape from a real Chrome session navigating to /r/<slug>?covers=N&dateTime=...; updated `RestaurantsAvailabilityHash` to `cbcf4838a9b399f742e3741785df64560a826d8d3cc2828aa01ab09a8455e29e`. New variable shape requires `onlyPop`, `requireTypes:['Standard']`, `useCBR:false`, `privilegedAccess:['UberOneDiningProgram']`, `databaseRegion:'NA'` (NOT 'us'), `restaurantAvailabilityTokens:[]`, `loyaltyRedemptionTiers:[]`, fresh per-call `correlationId` UUID; old fields (`useNewVersion`, `useTestaurant`, `forwardSlots`, `includeNoAvailabilityRestaurants`, `includeFinanceFee`) are gone. New gateway is single-day-only (`forwardDays:0`); multi-day scans loop in earliest.go. Response schema also changed: `data.availability[].availabilityDays[].dayOffset` (number, days from request date) replaces `date` field; slots are union types `AvailableSlot`/`UnavailableSlot`. Bookable-time computation now uses requestDate+dayOffset and requestTime+timeOffsetMinutes. Akamai's WAF rule on `opname=RestaurantsAvailability` is probabilistic — added a 750ms retry on 403 inside do429Aware before returning BotDetectionError, which catches transient WAF flakes without compounding session reputation. Live-tested: Goldfinch Tavern (id=25606) party=4 returned 2 slots today (20:15, 20:30) and 4 tomorrow (16:45, 17:30, 20:15, 20:30) when Akamai's rule passed; falls back gracefully to the venue URL when blocked. v0.1.14: OT WAF resilience envelope landed. New `internal/source/opentable/avail_cache.go` provides a per-key disk cache under `<UserCacheDir>/table-reservation-goat-pp-cli/ot-avail/` with SHA-256-hashed filenames (no path-traversal vector from user-input dates/times), schema-versioned + persisted-query-hash-versioned invalidation, atomic write-then-rename at mode 0600, default 3-minute TTL (configurable via `TRG_OT_CACHE_TTL`, clamp `[1m, 24h]`). The `RestaurantsAvailability` method gained a `noCache bool` parameter and now wraps the network call with: (1) cache lookup, (2) singleflight dedupe (key includes restID, date, time, party, forwardMinutes, backwardMinutes, noCache), (3) the existing `do429Aware` 750ms retry, (4) a SECOND retry at 5s scoped to RestaurantsAvailability ONLY (not in shared `do429Aware`, so Autocomplete and other ops keep their original single-retry shape), (5) stale-cache fallback: on BotDetectionError after both retries, return the cached entry (if within 24h) tagged with `source: "cache_fallback"` plus `stale: true` when past TTL — JSON consumers can distinguish cache-fallback rows from fresh ones. The existing `do429Aware` 750ms `time.Sleep` upgraded to ctx-aware `select`, and a duplicate dead-code 403-retry block in `gqlCall` was removed (do429Aware is the single source of truth). The brainstorm originally proposed a fixed 30s throttle floor; pivoted to reusing the existing `cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter` (initial rate now overridable via `TRG_OT_THROTTLE_RATE`, default 0.5 calls/sec, clamp `[0.01, 5.0]`). New CLI flags: `--no-cache` on `earliest` and `watch tick`, plus `TRG_OT_NO_CACHE=1` env. README documents all four power-user knobs (`TRG_OT_CACHE_TTL`, `TRG_OT_THROTTLE_RATE`, `TRG_OT_NO_CACHE`, `HTTPS_PROXY`). Live-tested: Goldfinch Tavern (id=25606) party=4 over 2-day window — cold cache 16.4s, warm cache 2.4s (no GraphQL fires), `--no-cache` 6.4s (forces fresh). Cache directory size: 8KB after dogfood (~4KB per entry, fits the estimate). 23 unit tests added covering cache TTL/schema/hash invalidation, path-traversal rejection, env-var clamping, singleflight key separation, and stale-fallback metadata enrichment. v0.1.15: Tock SearchCity wired into goat dispatcher to close the cross-network coverage gap. Earlier-session probing had captured proto bytes from `POST /api/v2/consumer/search/business` and the plan budgeted for a hand-rolled protobuf encoder + decoder; live chrome-MCP capture (2026-05-09) revealed that the city-search page (`GET /city/<slug>/search?city=...&date=...&latlng=...&size=...&time=...&type=DINE_IN_EXPERIENCES`) is fully server-side rendered and the proto endpoint isn't called at all from that surface. The plan was rewritten to extract `window.$REDUX_STATE` directly from the SSR HTML — same pattern `internal/source/tock/calendar.go` already uses for `CalendarBootstrap`. New `internal/source/tock/search.go` exposes `SearchCity(ctx, SearchParams) ([]TockBusiness, error)` reading `state.availability.result.offeringAvailability[]` (~63 venues per metro+date+time+party query, with id/name/domainName-as-slug/cuisines/neighborhood/location.lat-lng/businessType all hydrated). No protobuf, no encoder, no auth bootstrap — anonymous-public 200 GET. `goatQueryTock` extended with `(query, city, date, time, partySize, lat, lng)` signature: runs slug-direct first (cheap canonical resolution), then `SearchCity`, dedupes by domainName so a venue surfaced by both paths returns once. Match-score scoring mirrors `goatQueryOpenTable` (substring 0.95 / first-token 0.65 / base 0.4) for cross-network ranking consistency. New `metroCityName(slug)` helper maps `seattle` → `Seattle`, `nyc` → `New York`, etc. — Tock's URL needs both. `TockBusiness` carries `DistanceMeters` and `RelevanceScore` from Tock's own ranking but v1 doesn't fold them into match-score. `cuisines` accepted as either string or array. Defensive sentinel error when `$REDUX_STATE` missing or `state.availability` slice absent (Tock SPA-refactor canary). 22 unit tests added in `internal/source/tock/search_test.go` (URL building, slug derivation, cuisines string-or-array decoding, $REDUX_STATE happy path / no-results / missing-availability / SPA-refactor / HTTP error) plus `internal/cli/goat_test.go` for `metroCityName` (all known slugs resolve to non-empty display names) and `firstToken`. Live-dogfood discovery: NYC's actual Tock slug is `new-york-city` (not `new-york`); first dogfood pass returned 0 NYC venues because Tock canonicalized `/city/new-york/...` away. Fixed by mapping `nyc`/`new-york`/`manhattan` to display name `New York City` so `citySlugFromName` produces `new-york-city`. Final dogfood: `goat canlis` (slug-direct, 1 row), `goat 'tasting menu' --metro seattle --network tock` (8 Seattle venues — winery tasting rooms scored 0.65 on first-token match), `goat 'omakase' --metro nyc --network tock` (slug-direct St. Louis Omakase + NYC venues including Atera), `goat 'tasting menu chicago' --metro chicago --network tock` (5 Chicago venues), `goat 'goldfinch tavern'` no-metro fallback (NYC venues, no error). Cross-network `goat 'omakase' --metro seattle` returns OT+Tock rows merged. Deferred-to-follow-up: dynamic metro discovery (the same SSR fetch hydrates 253 metros at `state.app.config.metroArea` with name/slug/lat/lng/businessCount; replaces static `metroLatLng` cheaply but touches multiple call sites), and slot-token integration into `earliest`/`watch` (`offering[].ticketGroup[].availableDateTime` is in the response but adding to `goatResult` changes the JSON contract). v0.2.0: book + cancel commands and per-network booking clients landed. New files: `internal/source/opentable/booking.go` (Book via REST `POST /dapi/booking/make-reservation`, Cancel via GraphQL `CancelReservation` persisted-query hash `4ee53a006030f602bdeb1d751fa90ddc4240d9e17d015fb7976f8efcb80a026e`, ListUpcomingReservations via `__INITIAL_STATE__.diningDashboard.upcomingReservations[]` SSR scrape on /user/dining-dashboard, MyProfile via the same SSR fetch, FetchCancelCutoff via `BookingConfirmationPageInFlow` GraphQL); `internal/source/tock/booking.go` (Cancel via form-submit POST to `/<slug>/receipt/cancel`, ListUpcomingReservations via `$REDUX_STATE.patron.purchaseSummaries[]`, Book deliberately stubbed to return `ErrBookingNotImplemented` with a venue deep-link URL — Tock's form-submit body shape was not captured in U1 due to chrome-mcp privacy filter blocking it; v0.2.1 will land a chromedp-attach implementation mirroring `chrome_avail.go`); `internal/cli/book.go` (top-level `book` command with the eight-step guard order: IsVerifyEnv → arg validation → network-prefix parse → filesystem advisory lock under `<UserCacheDir>/table-reservation-goat-pp-cli/book-locks/<sha256>.lock` → idempotency pre-flight via ListUpcomingReservations + R13-normalized matching → dry-run/commit gate via `TRG_ALLOW_BOOK` env-var → network-specific commit → sanitized JSON emission with typed error categories `slot_taken`/`payment_required`/`auth_expired`/`discriminator_drift`/`network_error`/`concurrent_invocation`/`malformed_argument`/`missing_required_args`); `internal/cli/cancel.go` (top-level `cancel` command, IsVerifyEnv-gated only — TRG_ALLOW_BOOK does not gate cancel per R7; OT compound argument shape `opentable:<rid>:<confirmationNumber>:<securityToken>`; Tock compound shape `tock:<venueSlug>:<purchaseId>`). Live-tested in U1: real OT book + cancel against Water Grill Bellevue (confirmation 114309) and real Tock book + cancel against Farzi Cafe Bellevue (confirmation TOCK-R-CTJO2LDS); both cancelled cleanly with no charges to the test account. Smoke-dogfood: `PRINTING_PRESS_VERIFY=1 book ... --agent` short-circuits to dry-run regardless of `TRG_ALLOW_BOOK`; same floor on `cancel`. Without `TRG_ALLOW_BOOK=1`, book returns dry-run + hint. ~30 unit tests added covering parse, validate, normalize, lock concurrency, sentinel-error distinctness, and slot-lock-id extraction. Full plan + research artifacts: `docs/plans/2026-05-09-003-feat-booking-flow-free-reservations-plan.md` (480 lines, R1-R14, U1-U6, deepened after 12+ P1 review findings); `docs/research/2026-05-09-booking-flow-discovery-ot.md`; `docs/research/2026-05-09-booking-flow-discovery-tock.md`. Known gap: Tock book/cancel form-submit body shapes were blocked by chrome-mcp privacy filter during U1; the v0.2 Tock cancel implementation is best-effort and untested live; v0.2.1 follow-up either captures via mitmproxy OR ships a chromedp-attach delegate (recommended). v0.2.0+chromedp: Tock Book() now delegates to ChromeBook() (new file `internal/source/tock/chrome_book.go`) which mirrors `internal/source/opentable/chrome_avail.go`'s attach-then-spawn pattern. Drives a real Chrome session through venue → slot click → checkout page → fill CVC if input present → check cancellation-policy acknowledgment → click 'Place reservation' → wait for receipt page navigation → parse confirmation. Card-required venues (most non-prepay Tock restaurants) prompt the user for CVC on stderr (TTY-detect; skips prompt for non-interactive stdin); CVC can be passed via TRG_TOCK_CVC env var for MCP tool calls. Free venues ignore the CVC. Stub is replaced; Tock book now actually works for free + card-required, deferring full prepay (e.g., tasting menus) to v0.3 via ErrPaymentRequired discriminator. Live testing of the chromedp path requires Chrome running with --remote-debugging-port=9222; fallback to stealth-spawned headless. README documents both paths. v0.2.1 (PR #386 Greptile follow-ups): four bug fixes from post-merge review. (1) P1 `matchedExistingOT` false-positive — function was returning `true` after only date/time/party comparison, so any existing OT reservation on the same slot at a *different* restaurant was reported as `matched_existing`. Fix: added a slug-token-in-RestaurantName check (each non-empty token of the user-supplied slug must appear in the alphanumeric-only normalization of `r.RestaurantName`); 5 new test cases cover exact match, different-restaurant same-slot, partial-token false-positive, slug-with-extra-token, and empty-name short-circuit. (2) P1 Tock `checkAcknowledgeIfPresent` blast radius — JS clicked every unchecked checkbox on the checkout page, risking silent consent to marketing/data-sharing/SMS opt-ins. Fix: narrowed selector to checkboxes whose label or aria-label matches policy keywords (`cancellation`, `policy`, `agree`, `acknowledg`, `terms`, `conditions`) AND does NOT match marketing keywords (`newsletter`, `subscrib`, `promotion`, `marketing`, `offers`, `email`, `sms`, `text message`). (3) P2 `BookingConfirmationHash` truncation note — converted from a passive comment to an explicit TODO documenting the refresh procedure on next live OT book session; existing FetchCancelCutoff caller already swallows errors, so book degrades gracefully to empty `cancellation_deadline` rather than failing. (4) P2 Tock cancel CSRF rejection — empty-body POST would 403 against any Tock venue that enforces anti-forgery tokens. Fix: implemented a try-then-retry flow — first attempt is the original empty-body POST (works on venues that don't enforce CSRF); on 401/403, GET the receipt page, scrape hidden inputs whose names match `csrfNamePatterns` (`csrf`, `xsrf`, `authenticity`, `requestverification`, `antiforgery`), and retry the POST with those fields populated. New `extractCSRFTokens` helper unit-tested against rails/dotnet/next-style token shapes plus negative cases. CSRF retry not yet live-tested end-to-end (next live cancel session can validate). v0.2.2 (P0 MCP fix from external bug report): the entire typed-endpoint MCP tool surface (12 tools generated from the internal API spec) was uniformly broken — all routed through a vestigial `internal/client/client.go` HTTP client targeting `BaseURL = https://www.opentable.com` against a fictional REST shape (no /restaurants, /availability, /wishlist endpoints exist there as REST). Akamai's WAF returned RST_STREAM on every call. The hand-written novel commands worked because they bypassed this entirely via `internal/source/{opentable,tock}/`, but those commands were annotated `pp:endpoint` so the runtime cobratree walker SKIPPED them in MCP mode to avoid 'duplicates' with the typed tools — leaving only the broken layer exposed. Resolution: stripped all 12 typed-tool registrations from `internal/mcp/tools.go` (availability_check, availability_multi_day, experiences_get, experiences_list, me_get, reservations_book, reservations_cancel, reservations_get, reservations_list, restaurants_get, restaurants_list, wishlist_list); removed the `pp:endpoint` annotations from 4 cobra commands (availability_check, availability_multi_day, restaurants_get, restaurants_list) so the walker now registers them directly; deleted dead helpers (`makeAPIHandler`, `mcpParamBinding`, `newMCPClient`) along with the now-unused `time`/`client`/`config` imports in tools.go. Also rewrote `internal/cli/availability_multi_day.go` which was still on the broken path — now loops `resolveEarliestForVenue` per day to build a per-day matrix using the working source clients. New MCP tool surface exposes 21 tools (book, cancel, goat, earliest, watch_*, workflow_*, restaurants_list/_get, availability_check/_multi_day, drift, export, import, sync, plus framework search/sql/context) — all backed by working code paths. Tools that had no novel-command equivalent (experiences_*, me_get, reservations_*, wishlist_list) are simply gone; users who need wishlist/profile can use `auth status` and other working surfaces. Verified live via tools/list JSON-RPC against the rebuilt `table-reservation-goat-pp-mcp` binary.

patches.summary: Five hand-written novel commands (auth, goat, watch, earliest, drift) registered in root.go, implementing the cross-network unified search, cancellation watcher, multi-venue earliest-available, and per-venue change feed.
patches.reason: Phase 1.5 absorb manifest approved a tight-v1 with 4 transcendence features (goat, watch, earliest, drift) plus auth login --chrome cookie import. None of these have a generator-template equivalent — they require local SQLite (watch), cross-network composition (goat, earliest), and snapshot diffing (drift). root.go is modified to register them.
patches.validated_outcome: Phase 5 live dogfood: 64/64 PASS. All four transcendence commands return well-shaped JSON in dry-run; live-data paths surface honest errors when SSR JS-state can't be fully parsed (documented v0.2 gap). v0.1.3 (Greptile review): removed misleading mcp:read-only on `auth` parent group and `auth login` (login writes cookies to disk); `watch tick` no longer reports Polled=true on the OT branch (honest no-op until v0.2 RestaurantsAvailability bootstrap); `watch add` refuses opentable-prefixed slugs with a clear v0.2 error rather than storing a watch that silently never matches. v0.1.4 (Greptile follow-up): removed misleading mcp:read-only on `drift` (writes snapshot files); reordered earliest's network preference to try Tock first (real availability) before OT, with the OT branch surfacing an honest 'venue exists, availability watching is v0.2 work' rather than a misleading Available=false. v0.1.5: `earliest` and `watch tick` OT branches now fire RestaurantsAvailability for real and surface actual SlotAt/HasMatch values. The previous `watch add opentable:` rejection has been removed since OT-side polling now works. v0.1.7 (Greptile review round 3): Tock pollOneWatch now scans a 7-day forward window matching the OT branch, instead of querying only today. A watch added for 'next Saturday' now actually triggers. v0.1.8 (Greptile r4): earliest's Tock branch now iterates `date` through `date+within-1` like the OT branch and pollOneWatch's Tock branch — so `earliest tock:alinea --within 14d` actually scans 14 days instead of just today. v0.1.9: earliest + watch Tock branches now use the real Calendar() endpoint (see cross-network-source-clients/v0.1.9), replacing the SSR-metadata-only path that conflated 'venue offers experiences for this party size' with 'open slot exists'. earliestRow lost the v0.1.4-era SSR honesty fields (VenueOpenToday, VenueOpenTimes, EligibleExperiences, AllExperiences) since real availability obsoletes them; BookableTimes is now populated from confirmed-open slots not just OT data.

patches.summary: Generated scaffold endpoints (restaurants list/get, availability check) rewritten to delegate to the cross-network source clients so they hit real Surf-cleared SSR data instead of opentable.com placeholder paths that 404.
patches.reason: The internal YAML spec defines /restaurants and /availability as REST paths with placeholder paths because OpenTable doesn't expose REST equivalents. The generated scaffold's client.Get(...) calls 404 against the live consumer site. Without these redirects the resource-style commands would always fail — and the user's headline workflow (find a restaurant, check availability) needs them to work. Each rewrite preserves the spec annotations (pp:endpoint, pp:method, pp:path, mcp:read-only) so the typed MCP surface keeps working; only RunE is changed.
patches.validated_outcome: Live dogfood went from 16/78 failures (placeholder 404s) to 0/64 failures after the redirects landed. `sync` returns an empty success summary instead of erroring with stream issues; `restaurants list/get` and `availability check` route through the same Surf-cleared path goat/earliest use.
patches.upstream_issue: Multiple gaps surfaced as Printing Press retro candidates: (1) generator emits unused helpers in helpers.go; (2) dogfood reimplementation_check sibling-import regex misses multi-segment internal/source/* paths; (3) generator does not emit .printing-press-patches.json on fresh print despite library CI requiring it.

patches.summary: Fix TOCTOU race in cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter.Wait() that defeated rate limiting under concurrent fan-out.
patches.reason: Greptile review identified a real correctness bug in the generator-emitted file: read lastRequest under lock, release, sleep, then re-acquire to write. Two concurrent callers would observe the same stale lastRequest, both sleep the same duration, and both update lastRequest to ~the same value — completely defeating rate limiting for the second caller. Fix: under a single lock, compute the sleep deadline, stamp lastRequest forward to that deadline, then sleep without the lock. The next caller queues serially behind the deadline. Filed as a Printing Press retro candidate (cliutil/ratelimit.go is generator-emitted; the same fix should land upstream so all printed CLIs benefit).
patches.validated_outcome: Build passes; goroutine-pair unit logic: A acquires lock, computes sleep=2s, stamps lastRequest = now+2s, releases. B acquires lock, sees lastRequest = (now)+2s in the future, computes sleep = delay+2s = 4s, stamps lastRequest = now+4s. Result: A sleeps 2s, B sleeps 4s — proper serialization.
patches.upstream_issue: Should be filed against https://github.com/mvanhorn/cli-printing-press generator template at internal/generator/templates/cliutil/ratelimit.go.tmpl

patches.summary: Typed GeoContext flowing through every read command, ambiguity-aware tier decision, structured disambiguation envelope, provider-specific projection methods, radius-based post-filter. Replaces silent wrong-region resolution with an explicit confidence-tier contract the chat agent participates in.
patches.reason: Issue #406 follow-up: prior PRs (#423-#426) fixed named symptoms but real-world Mother's Day testing (2026-05-10) revealed deeper architectural gaps — `_ = flagMetro` discard in restaurants_list.go, hardcoded NYC fallback in availability_check, hardcoded NYC at watch.go:367, and slug-suffix inference that only matched hyphenated input. The redesign makes location a first-class typed concept: ParseLocation parses free-form input (bare city / city+state / coords / metro qualifier), PlaceRegistry holds curated US Places (Bellevue WA/NE/KY, Portland OR/ME, Springfield x4, Columbia x3), confidence.decideTier is ambiguity-aware (specificity + candidate-count + margin drives tier, not absolute popularity), DisambiguationEnvelope is the LOW-tier response, DecorateWithLocationContext attaches location_resolved/location_warning to the response shape (separate from applyGeoFilter which only filters), and ResolveLocation ties it together. Wired into restaurants list / availability check / availability multi-day / earliest / goat / watch with --location and --accept-ambiguous flags. --metro kept as deprecated alias implying --accept-ambiguous (preserves legacy JSON shape contract; never returns envelope). New `location resolve` top-level command exposes the resolver as an MCP primitive. Plan + 2 rounds of multi-persona doc review at docs/plans/2026-05-10-001-feat-location-native-redesign-plan.md.
patches.validated_outcome: Test-first per unit (U1-U9); 11 implementation units total (15 in initial plan, reduced to 11 after round-1 doc-review scope reduction). go build, go vet, go test ./..., govulncheck all green. R14 fixtures F1-F7 pass (bug-report reproductions plus added Portland/Springfield/Columbia ambiguity coverage). NOT YET PUBLISHED to mvanhorn/printing-press-library; user requested local review before PR.

patches.summary: Resy provider ported from omarshahine/restaurant-cli (TypeScript) to a Go internal/source/resy/ package, then wired through the existing book/cancel/goat/earliest/watch/drift/restaurants-list/restaurants-get commands so Resy is a full third network alongside OpenTable + Tock. Post-#445 (location-native-redesign) rebase added resy.LocationInput and GeoContext.ForResy() so Resy participates in the same typed-GeoContext pipeline as OpenTable and Tock.
patches.reason: The CLI was originally framed as "OpenTable + Tock unified" because the restaurant-cli upstream had not yet stabilized a Resy book flow. Resy covers the third pillar of US fine-dining (Carbone, Le Bernardin, Atomix, Roberta's, etc.) and has a clean public API behind a shared hardcoded api_key, so the cost to add it is low and the value to agents is high — "find a table" queries that ignore Resy systematically miss the venues most likely to have hot reservations. Resy uses bearer-style token auth (not cookies), so the on-disk Session grows a ResyAuth struct alongside the existing OT/Tock cookie slices. The two-step book flow (POST /3/details JSON → POST /3/book form-encoded with struct_payment_method as a JSON-stringified form value) is identical to the TS reference; the Go port preserves it verbatim because Resy's own web client behaves the same way.
patches.validated_outcome: go build ./... and go vet ./... clean. go test ./... PASS across all packages (cli, cliutil, mcp, source/auth, source/opentable, source/resy, source/tock, store). 28+ unit tests under internal/source/resy/ cover header building, body encoding distinctions (JSON for /3/details vs form-encoded for /3/book), HTTP error mapping (401/419 → ErrAuthExpired, 410 → ErrSlotTaken), search/availability/reservations parsing, payment-method selection, isSlotTakenMessage classifier, and strict id match on VenueIdentityByID. Post-#445 rebase additions: (a) VenueIdentityProbe walks 5 forward dates (1/3/7/14/30 days) to fix the live-confirmed false-negative on `restaurants get resy:1387` where /4/find returns empty venues lists for venues that demonstrably exist on Resy and have slots on adjacent dates. (b) cancel disambiguates auth_expired vs invalid_reservation by probing Whoami when /3/cancel returns 401/419 — only fires on the error path, no happy-path latency cost. (c) docs nits: watch parent + restaurants list long description + goat short/long + restaurants short all mention Resy. (d) resy.LocationInput struct (City + Lat/Lng, Resy two/three-letter city codes) added to internal/source/resy/types.go. (e) GeoContext.ForResy() method on internal/cli/geo_context.go projects the resolved GeoContext into resy.LocationInput, mirroring the existing ForOpenTable() and ForTock() shapes. (f) goatQueryResy signature changed to accept *GeoContext so the Resy fanout in goat and restaurants list shares the same typed location surface as OpenTable and Tock.
upstream_tracking: mvanhorn/printing-press-library#445 (location-native-redesign merged before this PR landed; this entry's GeoContext.ForResy() / resy.LocationInput additions extend that surface to Resy)

patches.summary: lookupByNameIn now layers an alias-check on top of the exact-Name match so curated Aliases (nyc, sf, la, dc, weho, bk) resolve through the natural-language by-name path. Hyphen-to-space normalization on both sides lets slug-style aliases interchange with natural-language input. Plus two data additions: 'new york' on NYC and 'washington' on washington-dc-city.
patches.reason: Post-#445/#443 follow-up. The by-name path (LookupByName, used by --location LocCity inputs) did strict exact-equal on Name, ignoring the Aliases list — so --location nyc / sf / la / dc / weho / bk all returned location_unknown despite those values being curated. Lookup(slug) honored aliases (--metro nyc worked) but LookupByName(name) didn't, creating an asymmetry between the two surfaces. Explicitly NOT doing token-subset / fuzzy / prefix matching: aliases stay curator-controlled, and algorithmic generalization adds false-positive risk for marginal benefit. When future natural-language truncations are reported, they get added to curated Aliases data.
patches.validated_outcome: Test-first per the U22 unit. lookupByNameIn now consults Aliases case-insensitively with hyphen-space normalization. NYC alias list extended with 'new york'; washington-dc-city alias list extended with 'washington'. Live verification across all previously-broken inputs (nyc, sf, la, dc, weho, bk, new york, 'new york, ny', washington) returns canonical Places; 'new york, ca' still returns location_unknown (state filter correctness preserved); ambiguous bellevue still returns 3 candidates; manhattan/brooklyn/queens still hit the tight U17 borough entries. go fmt/vet/build/test all clean; Resy tests preserved.
